signal_title: FTC's Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees
risk_rationale: The new FTC rule on unfair or deceptive fees introduces requirements that may affect TikTok's e-commerce and advertising practices, particularly in the context of live-event ticketing and short-term lodging. The risk is moderate as it requires adjustments to pricing transparency mechanisms.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Price transparency: All-in pricing including duties/taxes
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Prohibits bait-and-switch pricing for live-event tickets and short-term lodging
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline requires all-in pricing but does not explicitly address bait-and-switch tactics or specific sectors like live-event ticketing and short-term lodging.

remediations.title: Enhance Pricing Transparency for Specific Sectors
remediations.description: Implement additional checks and disclosures to ensure compliance with FTC's rule on unfair or deceptive fees, specifically targeting live-event ticketing and short-term lodging sectors.
acceptance_criteria: All pricing displays for live-event tickets and short-term lodging must include total costs upfront.
Implement a review process to identify and eliminate any bait-and-switch pricing tactics.

business_impact: The rule may require changes to how prices are displayed in certain sectors, potentially impacting user experience and conversion rates if not implemented carefully.
recommended_actions: Conduct a review of current pricing practices in live-event ticketing and short-term lodging.
Develop and deploy enhanced pricing transparency features.
Train relevant teams on the new regulatory requirements to ensure ongoing compliance.
